rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Monica Zimmerman To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is MOTION BY MONICA ZIMMERMAN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SUPPLEMENTAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS. The court GRANTS moving party's request for judicial notice. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Moving party's request for an order to compel Rudolph to provide a further complete, code-compliant substantive response, without objection, to Monica's First Supplemental Interrogatory, on the grounds that the objection stated in Rudolph's currently operative "Rudolph Koppl's Supplemental Responses to Monica Zimmerman's Supplemental Interrogatories, Set One" (re Form Interrogatories), and Rudolph Koppl's Supplemental Responses to Monica Zimmerman's Supplemental Interrogatories, Set One (re Specially Prepared Interrogatories), both served on June 16, 2022, are both procedurally and substantively improper, as the stated objection lacks merit and is too general is DENIED. Moving party's request for an order to compel Rudolph to provide further complete, correct, and straightforward supplemental responses to Form Interrogatory no. 17.1 of Monica's First Set of Form Interrogatories, General, served on October 3, 2018, with respect to Request for Admissions nos. 1 through 7, 16 through 29, 37 through 39, 45, 62, 67, and 68 is DENIED. Moving party's request for an order to compel Rudolph to provide further full and complete responses with respect to Interrogatory nos. 20 through 24, and 38 of Respondent Monica Zimmerman's First Set of Specially Prepared Interrogatories Addressed to Petitioner Rudolph Koppl; Declaration in Support of Additional Discovery, Set One served on October 3, 2018 is DENIED. Moving party's request for award of monetary sanctions against Rudolph and against his attorney Andrew Zabronsky under Code Civ. Proc. sections 2023.010 et seq. and 2030.300 on the grounds that there was no substantial justification for Rudolph's failure to provide further correct and complete responses to Monica's First Supplemental Interrogatory in regard to the interrogatories identified above, without the improper general objection is DENIED. The responses to Form Interrogatory no. 17.1 of Monica's First Set of Form Interrogatories, General, served on October 3, 2018, with respect to Request for Admissions nos. 1 through 7, 16 through 29, 37 through 39, 45, 62, 67, and 68, and responses with respect to Interrogatory nos. 20 through 24, and 38 of Respondent Monica Zimmerman's First Set of Specially Prepared Interrogatories Addressed to Petitioner Rudolph Koppl; Declaration in Support of Additional Discovery, Set One served on October 3, 2018, complained of in this motion appear to be code-compliant. The motion's arguments otherwise are not persuasive. Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.300, in an amount of no more than $15,000 total, upon further showing of relevant hourly billing rate(s) and how billed time was spent on the three motions currently before the court. The court does not find that Monica acted with substantial justification in bringing this motion to compel or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Monica Zimmerman, as moving party, is ordered to pay Rudolph Koppl sanctions in an amount to be determined by the court, within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Rodolph Koppl is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. [Part 1 of 2]

Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Alan Zimmerman To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is MOTION BY ALAN ZIMMERMAN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SUPPLEMENTAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS. The court GRANTS moving party's request for judicial notice. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Moving party's request for an order to compel Rudolph to provide a further complete, code-compliant substantive response, without objection, to Alan's First Supplemental Interrogatory, on the grounds that the objection stated in Rudolph's currently operative Rudolph Koppl's Supplemental Responses to Alan Zimmerman's Supplemental Interrogatories, Set One [re Form Interrogatories] and Rudolph Koppl's Supplemental Responses to Alan Zimmerman's Supplemental Interrogatories, Set One [re Specially Prepared Interrogatories], both served on June 16, 2022, are both procedurally and substantively improper as the stated objection lacks merit and is too general is DENIED. Moving party's request for an order to compel Rudolph to provide a further complete, correct, and straightforward supplemental responses to Form Interrogatory no. 17.1 of Alan's First Set of Form Interrogatories, General, served on December 31, 2020, with respect to Request for Admissions nos. 25 through 28 and 31 through 38, on the grounds that they are evasive and/or incomplete because they fail to include "later acquired information bearing on all answers previously made " Code Civ. Proc. section 2030.070(a) is DENIED. Moving party's request for award of monetary sanctions against Rudolph and against his attorney Andrew Zabronsky under Code Civ. Proc. sections 2023.010 et seq. and 2030.300 on the grounds that there was no substantial justification for Rudolph's failure to provide further correct and complete responses to Alan's First Supplemental Interrogatory in regard to the interrogatories identified above, without the improper general objection is DENIED. The responses to Form Interrogatory no. 17.1 of Alan's First Set of Form Interrogatories, General, served on December 31, 2020, with respect to Request for Admissions nos. 25 through 28 and 31 through 38, and the objection stated in Rudolph's currently operative Rudolph Koppl's Supplemental Responses to Alan Zimmerman's Supplemental Interrogatories, Set One [re Form Interrogatories] and Rudolph Koppl's Supplemental Responses to Alan Zimmerman's Supplemental Interrogatories, Set One [re Specially Prepared Interrogatories], both served on June 16, 2022, and complained of in this motion appear to be code-compliant. The motion's arguments otherwise are not persuasive. Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.300, in an amount of no more than $15,000 total, upon further showing of relevant hourly billing rate(s) and how billed time was spent on the three motions currently before the court. The court does not find that Alan acted with substantial justification in bringing this motion to compel or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Alan Zimmerman, as moving party, is ordered to pay Rudolph Koppl sanctions in an amount to be determined by the court, within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Rodolph Koppl is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Monica Zimmerman To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is MOTION BY MONICA ZIMMERMAN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SUPPLEMENTAL REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS. The court GRANTS moving party's request for judicial notice. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Moving party's request for an order of this court under Code Civ. Proc. section 2031.310 to compel Rudolph to provide a further full, complete, code-compliant substantive response, without objection, to Monica Zimmerman's First Supplemental Request for Inspection and Production of Documents and Things [Monica's Supplemental RFPD], on the grounds that the objection stated in Rudolph's currently operative Rudolph Koppl's Supplemental Responses to Monica Zimmerman's Supplemental Request for Inspection and Production of Documents and Things, Set One is both procedurally and substantively improper, as it lacks merit and is too general is DENIED. The responses in Rudolph Koppl's Supplemental Responses to Monica Zimmerman's Supplemental Request for Inspection and Production of Documents and Things, Set One, complained of in this motion appear to be code-compliant. The motion's arguments otherwise are not persuasive. Moving party's request for an award of monetary sanctions against Rudolph and against his attorney Andrew Zabronsky under Code Civ. Proc. sections 2023.010 et seq. and 2030.300 on the grounds that there was no substantial justification for Rudolph's failure to provide further correct and complete responses to Monica's Supplemental RFPD without the improper general objection is DENIED. Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.300, in an amount of no more than $15,000 total, upon further showing of relevant hourly billing rate(s) and how billed time was spent on the three motions currently before the court. The court does not find that Monica acted with substantial justification in bringing this motion to compel or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Monica Zimmerman, as moving party, is ordered to pay Rudolph Koppl sanctions in an amount to be determined by the court, within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Rodolph Koppl is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. [Part 1 of 2]
